findings.title: Mobile crash reporting is dead in shipped builds — zero Sentry telemetry from the primary surface in 90 days
findings.detail: Live Sentry queries (evidence/sentry-issues.md) show the super-mobile project has received ZERO events AND zero spans in 90 days — verified five ways. Root cause confirmed this pass: apps/mobile/lib/errorTracking.ts:6-7 resolves the DSN from Constants.expoConfig.extra.sentryDsn ?? EXPO_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN; apps/mobile/app.json's extra block (lines 98-106) has no sentryDsn key; EXPO_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN exists only in the gitignored root .env.local (line 93); and `eas env:list production` returns 'No variables found for this environment.' So every EAS/TestFlight build resolves DSN to "" and the SDK no-ops. Additionally eas.json sets SENTRY_DISABLE_AUTO_UPLOAD=true on both build profiles, so mobile source maps are also off. The iOS app is the PRIMARY product surface — if it crashes for launch users, nothing tells anyone.
findings.recommendation: Add the DSN to the EAS production environment (`eas env:create --environment production --name EXPO_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN --value <dsn>` — a DSN is public, so app.json extra.sentryDsn is also acceptable), wire SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN as an EAS secret and remove SENTRY_DISABLE_AUTO_UPLOAD so source maps upload, ship a new TestFlight build, then force one test crash and confirm it lands in super-mobile before any launch traffic.

findings.title: The one wired security alarm (Supabase advisor → Sentry) has failed silently every day since 2026-07-02 — SUPABASE_PAT unset in Vercel production
findings.detail: The daily supabase-advisor-check cron 503s with {"error":"server_misconfigured","message":"SUPABASE_PAT unset"} on all 3 retries, every day since the 2026-07-01 GH-Actions cron migration. Re-verified live today: `gh run list` shows the 06:00-UTC slot failing again at 2026-07-05T07:02:56Z (2m7s = 3 retries) while hourly slots pass, and `vercel env ls production` confirms SUPABASE_PAT is still absent. Worse, the failure is itself invisible — schedule-triggered GitHub Actions failures do not email by default, so RLS/security advisor regressions have zero automated visibility and nobody knows the watcher is dark. This is the project's own most-repeated historical failure mode (monitoring gap unnoticed for weeks) recurring inside the system built to prevent it.
findings.recommendation: Grace creates a Supabase personal access token, runs `vercel env add SUPABASE_PAT production`, and redeploys (env vars need a redeploy per the webhook runbooks). Then add an `if: failure()` step to .github/workflows/scheduled-crons.yml that notifies on any cron failure (simplest: `sentry-cli send-event` or an email action to gracehowse@outlook.com) so a dark watcher can never be silent again.
evidenceRefs: live: gh run list run 28732775129 failure 2026-07-05T07:02:56Z; vercel env ls production (no SUPABASE_PAT)

findings.title: Data plane: Supabase Free plan, 24h RPO, restore never rehearsed, PITR decision blank for 5 weeks, storage bytes unmirrored — and the tracking ticket is closed
findings.detail: Live-verified 2026-07-05: org plan is `free` (MCP get_organization), so backup posture is a daily logical backup with up to 24h data loss and no PITR. The restore has never been executed or timed (runbook's own words: 'a restore that has never been rehearsed is a restore that doesn't exist'). docs/decisions/2026-06-01-pitr-posture.md's Decision section is still blank. The recipe-images bucket (117 objects, ~48MB, live-queried) has zero bytes-level redundancy. Linear ENG-510 is marked Done (2026-06-02) while its own checklist items 'Confirm PITR enabled' and 'Run restore drill' are unchecked — the silent-deferral pattern CLAUDE.md prohibits. Pre-Phase-1 checklist: 3 of 9 done; the three most consequential (rehearsal, plan decision, storage mirror) open, a month past deadline.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade the Supabase org to Pro and enable PITR (Option A in docs/decisions/2026-06-01-pitr-posture.md — fill in the Decision section same day); run one timed restore rehearsal to a scratch project and record it in the runbook's rehearsal log table; add a nightly recipe-images mirror job (scheduled GH Actions cron copying new storage objects to a second bucket or external store); reopen ENG-510 or open a successor ticket so the tracker matches reality. Owner: Grace (spend decision) + Claude (rehearsal + mirror). Deadline: before first public traffic.

findings.title: Both core infra vendors are on free tiers into a viral launch — Supabase Free hard-stops, Vercel Hobby can pause the deployment, and no alarm would catch either
findings.detail: Supabase Free (live-confirmed) does not degrade at plan limits — it hard-stops: 500MB DB → read-only (signups and food logs fail product-wide), ~5GB/month egress that the photo-log/recipe-image round-trips could exceed in a single 10k-user day, MAU gating on auth. Vercel is confirmed Hobby (crons had to move to GH Actions because Hobby rejects sub-daily schedules): Hobby's terms restrict commercial use and Vercel can pause a project exceeding fair use — the first signal could be a paused deployment, not an invoice. Zero of the alerting runbook's alarms detect plan-limit proximity on either vendor, and Alarm 5 (Vercel error rate) cannot even be configured on Hobby. Running a planned 10k-users-in-a-day push on this footing risks a total, undetected outage at the exact moment of maximum visibility.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade Vercel to Pro and Supabase to Pro before driving any launch traffic (this also unblocks PITR from PRA-003 and Alarm 5). Immediately after upgrading: enable Vercel's usage + error-rate notifications to gracehowse@outlook.com, and enable Supabase's usage/billing email alerts (DB size, egress, MAU) in the dashboard. Owner: Grace (payment). Deadline: before the viral push resumes.

findings.title: Minimum alarm set still unwired 7 weeks after the alerting doc's own 'zero alarms' audit — Stripe webhook failure, Sentry quota/spike, Vercel error rate, PostHog heartbeat
findings.detail: Only 3 of 12 catalogued alarms are wired (PostHog event-cap — genuinely production-grade; AI budget 70%; the now-broken advisor cron). Live-verified: Sentry has only its auto-provisioned default rule per project (no volume condition, routes to ActiveMembers fallthrough, not a named recipient — works today only because Grace is the sole org member); PostHog error-tracking has zero outbound destinations; app_heartbeat has never fired and has zero code references, despite being written up as THE fix for the 2026-04-21→05-11 three-week silent PostHog outage. Stripe webhook delivery failure — the billing-grade alarm — is unwired, meaning entitlement drift after a bad deploy would go unnoticed until a paying user complains.
findings.recommendation: One afternoon of wiring, in this order: (1) Stripe Dashboard → Webhooks → endpoint → enable failing-webhook email notifications to gracehowse@outlook.com; (2) Sentry org Settings → Spend Notifications at 70% and 90% of quota; (3) replace the default alert rule in both Sentry projects with an authored rule 'new issue OR >50 events/1hr → email gracehowse@outlook.com' per Alarm 1's spec; (4) implement app_heartbeat: add a 15-min GH Actions cron POSTing a synthetic PostHog event plus a PostHog alert on 'app_heartbeat count == 0 over 2h'. The alerting.md dry-run script already documents how to test all of them.
evidenceRefs: live: Sentry find_alert_rules (rules 3436623/3436617); PostHog error-tracking-alerts-list count:0

findings.title: Solo-founder safety net: recovery vault not populated and trusted contact not named — 3 weeks past its own blocker deadline
findings.detail: docs/operations/founder-safety-net.md §5 status checklist: 'Recovery codes stored in a documented vault' and 'Trusted contact identified, briefed, conditional access' are both unchecked (doc last updated 2026-06-02; ENG-514 deadline was 2026-06-15). If Grace loses her laptop or phone today, there is no documented recovery path into Apple/App Store Connect, Stripe, Supabase, Vercel, GitHub, or the domain — every one is a single point of failure and the loss of Apple ID 2FA alone could strand the app. The playbook and vendor inventory are excellent; the two human actions that make them real never happened. This is the exact scenario the dimension exists for and no code change can substitute.
findings.recommendation: Grace (founder-gated, non-delegable, ~2 hours): create a 1Password vault, populate all 11 systems from the §1 table (login + 2FA seed/backup codes + recovery codes each), print the Emergency Kit and vault recovery key, store physically; name one trusted contact, brief them on the §4 playbook, and configure 1Password Emergency Access with a 48h wait. Tick the ENG-514 boxes when done.

findings.title: Privacy policy asserts signed DPAs that do not exist, and fal.ai plus other live sub-processors are missing from the public table; VAT disclosure is dark in production
findings.detail: docs/legal/dpa-inventory.md states plainly that no vendor DPA is in Signed state, while app/privacy/page.tsx claims each sub-processor 'is bound by a data-processing agreement' — a live false statement on a public legal page. The public sub-processor table omits fal.ai (which receives user recipe/ingredient text), Anthropic, Supadata, Upstash, and Vercel. Separately, verified live this pass: STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED is absent from Vercel production, so the ratified UK/EU VAT-inclusive disclosure (2026-04-19 consumer-VAT posture) renders nothing — conservative (no false claim) but the required disclosure is not live, and verify-production-env.ts doesn't check the var. Note Grace's standing rule that legal engages ~2d pre-launch and never blocks the build — but the stated launch date (2026-07-01) has passed, so this is now due, not deferred. Routing to legal-reviewer.
findings.recommendation: Execute the self-serve DPAs (Supabase, Vercel, OpenAI, Anthropic, PostHog, Sentry, Stripe all offer standard click-through DPAs — most are <15 min each), update app/privacy/page.tsx's sub-processor table to include fal.ai and the other omitted vendors in the same change, then configure Stripe Tax in inclusive mode, set STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED=true in Vercel production (+ redeploy), and add STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED to scripts/verify-production-env.ts so it can never silently regress.
evidenceRefs: live: vercel env ls production (no STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED)

findings.title: Sentry environment hygiene: dev and local traffic pollute the production project — 100% of 'production'-tagged web errors over 90 days are local smoke tests
findings.detail: Every unresolved suppr-web issue traces to localhost:3000, 127.0.0.1:3100, HeadlessChrome, or Grace's own MacBook (server_name GTs-MacBook-Air.local); the 91 'production'-tagged events are entirely one local prod-build preview artifact (Vercel insights script 404 on /login). Real deployed-production web traffic is near-silent in Sentry. When launch traffic arrives, real incidents will be buried in dev noise and triage speed (the '<5 minutes from a TestFlight report' bar) degrades. Also: the server-only SENTRY_DSN var was never set (server/edge fall back to the public DSN — works today, fragile against SDK changes).
findings.recommendation: Set `environment` explicitly from VERCEL_ENV in instrumentation-client.ts, sentry.server.config.ts, and sentry.edge.config.ts (dev machines get 'development', local prod-preview gets 'local-preview', deployed prod gets 'production'), and add a Sentry inbound filter or per-project rule excluding non-production environments from alerting. Set SENTRY_DSN in Vercel production while in there. Then resolve the 5 stale dev issues so the unresolved list starts clean for launch.

findings.title: PostHog session replay defaults to 100% capture; the documented dashboard flip to 0.1 is unverified — fastest silent quota burn in the stack
findings.detail: src/lib/analytics/sessionReplaySampleRate.ts hardcodes DEFAULT_SESSION_REPLAY_SAMPLE_RATE = 1.0 with a doc comment planning a dashboard-flag flip to 0.1 pre-launch. Whether that flip happened is unverifiable from the repo — if not, every session ships full-fidelity replay. A 10k-user day at 100% replay + ~30 autocapture events/session burns ~30% of a 1M-events/month cap in 24 hours, and PostHog cuts replay FIRST at the hard cap — silently, exactly when launch-week bug reports need replays most. Mobile compounds it: the sample rate is cached in AsyncStorage and only updates on next app launch.
findings.recommendation: Set the session-replay sample-rate flag payload to 0.1 in the PostHog dashboard now (no deploy needed; mobile picks it up on next launch), verify via the flag's live payload, and note the change in docs/decisions/2026-05-13-session-replay-and-feature-flags.md. Keep the (already-wired) 70% event-cap alarm as the backstop.

findings.title: AI and image-gen budget caps are sized for 1k DAU, not the launch profile — and the enforcement flag's production value is unverifiable
findings.detail: The AI circuit breaker (£50/day global, 50 calls/user/day, 70% alarm) is the best-engineered guardrail in the stack, but its own sizing doc computes headroom for 1k DAU; 10k DAU ≈ £250/day, tripping the cap within ~2 hours by design. fal.ai's £10/day cap ≈ 90 images — 5% of 10k users importing one recipe trips it 5× over on day one. Verified this pass: AI_BUDGET_ENFORCEMENT_ENABLED exists in Vercel production, created 18 days ago — exactly matching the 2026-06-17 ENG-1158 flip decision — but it is a sensitive var (pulls as empty) so the value 'true' could not be confirmed from CLI. If it is actually off, a viral day burns real uncapped spend instead of hitting the designed 503.
findings.recommendation: Grace opens Vercel → Settings → Environment Variables and eyeballs AI_BUDGET_ENFORCEMENT_ENABLED=true (2 minutes — closes the 2026-06-22 audit's P1-9 for good). Then pre-set launch-day caps deliberately rather than mid-incident: raise AI_BUDGET_GLOBAL_DAILY_GBP and FAL_BUDGET_DAILY_GBP to values Grace is willing to spend on a viral day (e.g. £150/£30), record the numbers in docs/decisions/2026-05-14-ai-cost-circuit-breaker.md, and rely on the 70% alarms for the rest.
evidenceRefs: live: vercel env ls/pull production (var present, sensitive, created ~2026-06-17)

findings.title: Food-vendor quota cliff: Edamam's 1,000/day cap is decorative at any real scale; FatSecret and USDA exhaust on a viral day
findings.detail: Edamam free tier (1,000 req/day shared) is exhausted by ~500 lookups — guaranteed degraded out of the source chain within the first hour of any real traffic, every day. FatSecret's 10,000/day cap equals one call per user on a 10k-user day with zero headroom; USDA's ~900/hr trip point exhausts in under 20 minutes of a novel-query spike. The degradation path is genuinely graceful (verified: 90% trip → degraded envelope → fall through to next vendor, 24h cache absorbing repeats), so search won't break — but nutrition-match quality silently narrows exactly when first impressions matter, and nothing makes exhaustion visible. The USDA 1,000/hr assumption also carries no dated re-verification.
findings.recommendation: Three concrete actions: (1) purchase Edamam's paid tier ($0.00003/request, no daily limit — already cited in src/lib/edamam/client.ts:7) or remove Edamam from the chain and document it; (2) re-verify the live USDA key's actual rate limit against the FDC account page and update VENDOR_QUOTAS.usda; (3) create a PostHog insight + alert on vendor-quota degraded events so vendor exhaustion emails Grace instead of silently narrowing match quality.

findings.title: Two structural scale bugs on the hot path: unbounded `saves` fetch on every session boot, and unindexed per-ingredient ilike scan in the core nutrition matcher
findings.detail: (1) The saves table is fetched with no .limit()/.range() on every authenticated boot on web (src/context/AppDataContext.tsx:1664-1667) and mobile (apps/mobile/lib/recipes.ts:303-306, 466-470, 516-524) while Base/Pro is explicitly unlimited-saves — payload grows without bound for exactly the retained-paying cohort the product optimizes for, and no pagination UX exists to retrofit. (2) user_foods.name is queried with a leading-wildcard ilike once per ingredient (up to 8 concurrent) inside verifyIngredients (src/lib/nutrition/verifyIngredients.ts:627), with no pg_trgm/GIN index — a sequential scan on a table whose entire purpose is to grow via crowdsourced contribution, sitting in the single highest-value nutrition path (every AI log and recipe import). Both degrade smoothly and invisibly — the hardest failure shape to catch later.
findings.recommendation: Ship two bounded fixes now while tables are small: (a) a migration enabling pg_trgm plus `CREATE INDEX ... ON user_foods USING gin (name gin_trgm_ops)` via `supabase db push --linked`, then EXPLAIN ANALYZE to confirm the planner uses it; (b) add .limit() with a cursor contract to the four saves hydrate call sites and a matching incremental-load seam client-side. File both as Linear issues under Platform foundations with the launch-blocker label on (a).

findings.title: Auth outage has no runbook — Sign in with Apple is the only mobile auth path and an Apple-side SIWA degradation has zero written response
findings.detail: The runbook census found no scenario for an auth-specific outage: DR runbook S7 covers Supabase-regional-infra only. Two uncovered failure domains: Apple's own SIWA service degrading (Suppr doesn't control it; it would fully lock out every mobile user since SIWA is the only mobile auth path per apps/mobile/CLAUDE.md), and Supabase Auth degrading while Postgres stays up (a distinct managed service). No trigger recognition, no comms template, no fallback — Grace would be diagnosing from scratch at 3am while users see a broken login.
findings.recommendation: Write scenario S8 in docs/runbooks/disaster-recovery.md: trigger recognition (check Apple System Status page + Supabase Auth status before assuming a Suppr bug; add a Sentry fingerprint for SIWA token-exchange failures), blast radius (mobile 100% locked out, web email-auth unaffected), response (flip a dr-auth-outage variant of the outage banner via PostHog flag; comms template pointing mobile users at web login as interim), and exit criteria. One page, ~1 hour.

findings.title: Last night's full CI run is red (6 timeout failures) and Vercel deploys are not gated on CI at all
findings.detail: The stage-1 ci-report.txt shows the chain died at web tests: 6 failures across 4 files (stripeCheckoutRoute, dayStripPagerStrideWeb ×3, foodSearchFitThisIn, onboardingSignupSessionGate), all 'Test timed out in 5000ms' — the known CPU-contention flake signature, but unproven; build + all mobile gates never ran. Separately, the runbook census confirmed Vercel's GitHub integration deploys on push to main independently of the Actions run — a red CI does not block a production deploy (ci.yml has no deploy step; the coupling doesn't exist). Together: the last full local gate is red AND the deploy pipeline wouldn't have cared.
findings.recommendation: Re-run the 4 failing files in isolation (`npm run test -- <file>` sequentially, no concurrent suites) to confirm flake vs regression, and fix or properly quarantine with a Linear ID. Then couple deploys to CI: add an ignored-build-step or use Vercel's 'require checks to pass' git integration setting so a red required check blocks the production deploy — the 60-second rollback is great, but not deploying a broken build is better.

findings.title: TestFlight 90-day build expiry is guarded only by a calendar reminder — no system alarm
findings.detail: Builds auto-expire after 90 days regardless of severity; if the only promoted build expires, there is no install path and no rollback target. The founder-safety-net policy ('never let the promoted build exceed ~75 days') is enforced solely by a recurring 60-day calendar event set 2026-06-02. Alarm 10 (weekly App Store Connect API poll on expirationDate) is designed in alerting.md but not built. A calendar reminder is exactly the mechanism that fails during a busy launch month or a 7-day absence — the two scenarios where it matters most. Tail-risk today at N=1; real cliff once external testers exist.
findings.recommendation: Build Alarm 10: add a weekly job to .github/workflows/scheduled-crons.yml calling the App Store Connect API (GET /v1/builds, the .p8 key from scripts/fetch-testflight-feedback.mjs's existing auth) and failing loudly (which, after PRA-002's failure-notification step lands, emails Grace) if the newest build's expirationDate is <21 days out.

findings.title: Dependency posture: 14 (web) + 23 (mobile) moderate advisories, none high/critical; posthog-js pinned to a vulnerable OpenTelemetry range
findings.detail: Ran `npm audit --omit=dev` fresh on both trees this pass. Web: 14 moderate — headlined by @opentelemetry/core <2.8.0 (unbounded memory allocation in W3C Baggage propagation, GHSA-8988-4f7v-96qf) pulled transitively through posthog-js 1.319.0–1.379.0, plus a moderate next advisory via @sentry/nextjs and @vercel/analytics. Mobile: 23 moderate, largely @expo/config-plugins transitive chains (react-native-health et al.). No high/critical on production paths. Expo SDK 54 is a supported version. Gitleaks runs in CI (ci.yml:223-229) — secrets scanning is genuinely wired. This dimension is soft, not broken: the memory-allocation advisory sits in the analytics hot path and deserves closing before sustained public traffic.
findings.recommendation: Run `npm audit fix` in both trees, bump posthog-js past 1.379.0, and re-run the full gate. Then add a monthly dependency-audit reminder (or enable Dependabot alerts on the repo, which is one settings toggle) so advisories surface without a manual audit.

findings.title: Web production PostHog is dead — NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY missing from Vercel prod (net-new, verified live this pass)
findings.detail: The deployed web app cannot initialise PostHog. src/app/components/AnalyticsProvider.tsx:61 gates posthog.init on process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY with no fallback (no hardcoded phc_ key, no next.config mapping — verified by grep); src/lib/analytics/track.ts:8,514 no-ops track() and returns false from isFeatureEnabled without it; src/lib/analytics/serverTrack.ts:69 no-ops server-side events, including the RC webhook's subscription_purchased/renewed events that were added specifically because mobile client events get lost. `vercel env ls production` (this pass) shows only NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST and NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_PROJECT_TOKEN — the name the code reads is absent. Consequences: zero web analytics, zero web session replay, every non-build-time-defaulted feature flag reads OFF on web prod (flag-gated UI renders the else path), and the one production-grade alarm in the whole stack (PostHog event-cap, last_value 259/mo) is metering a surface that isn't reporting. This is the exact recurrence of the 2026-04-21→05-11 silent PostHog outage class; the app_heartbeat alarm written up as its fix (Alarm 7) was never implemented (zero repo hits, event never fired).
findings.recommendation: Today: add NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY=phc_yCobaLpX93MdmGkLDM33xKr5DRvUx2JaGJvjJ7jUR79E (the same token already baked into apps/mobile/app.json:103) to Vercel Production+Preview, verify NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST resolves to the /ingest first-party proxy (not a direct posthog host), redeploy, and confirm one event lands in project 389168 from the suppr.app domain. This week: implement the app_heartbeat synthetic event (Alarm 7 spec in docs/operations/alerting.md) fired by the existing scheduled-crons workflow, plus a PostHog absence alert ('0 app_heartbeat events in 6h' insight alert emailed to gracehowse@outlook.com) so this class can never silently recur a third time.
evidenceRefs: audit2 evidence/observability.md §5 (Alarm 7 never fired, event-cap last_value 259)

findings.title: Zero Stripe env vars in Vercel production — web checkout and Stripe webhook hard-503; web billing is entirely dark (net-new, verified live this pass)
findings.detail: The full `vercel env ls production` list (49 vars, captured this pass) contains no STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, no STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, no STRIPE_PRICE_BASE_MONTHLY/STRIPE_PRICE_PRO_MONTHLY, and no STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED. app/api/stripe/checkout/route.ts:58-59 returns 503 stripe_not_configured, so a web user tapping Upgrade on /pricing gets a dead checkout; app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts hard-503s by design, so any dashboard-configured webhook endpoint would fail 100% — with Alarm 4 (webhook delivery failure) also unwired, that failure would be silent. STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED absent also means the UK/EU VAT-inclusive disclosure (docs/decisions/2026-04-19-consumer-vat-posture-uk-eu.md) is dark on both the pricing page and the billing status card, and scripts/verify-production-env.ts doesn't check that var at all (confirmed zero grep hits). The handler code itself is excellent (signature verification, DB-backed idempotency, correct 500-for-retry, lifetime_pro floor) — the integration is simply not connected to production. Mobile RevenueCat is the only live billing rail.
findings.recommendation: Decision for Grace, then a 30-minute fix either way. Option A (Stripe launches with the push): create the live-mode webhook endpoint (POST /api/stripe/webhook, events per verify-production-env output: checkout.session.completed + customer.subscription.created/updated/deleted), set the 4 Stripe vars + STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED in Vercel prod, redeploy, run one test-clock checkout end-to-end. Option B (web billing intentionally deferred pending incorporation/Stripe Atlas): gate the web upgrade CTA to an honest 'iOS-only for now' state in the same commit and record the deferral in docs/decisions/ + a Linear ticket — a 503 behind a live Buy button is not a valid deferral. Either way: add STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED to scripts/verify-production-env.ts strict checks.
evidenceRefs: live `vercel env ls production` full list (this pass)

findings.title: Mobile Sentry has received zero events in 90 days — shipped TestFlight builds have no DSN; a 3am crash on the PRIMARY surface is invisible
findings.detail: Live Sentry data (audit2 evidence/sentry-issues.md, verified 5 ways) shows the super-mobile project has 0 errors and 0 spans over 90 days — no telemetry at all, not 'no bugs'. Root cause traced this pass: apps/mobile/lib/errorTracking.ts resolves the DSN from Constants.expoConfig?.extra?.sentryDsn ?? EXPO_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN ?? "", with enabled: !__DEV__ (line 21) — so release builds ARE enabled but with an empty DSN. apps/mobile/app.json bakes posthogKey into extra (line 103) but has no sentryDsn key, and apps/mobile/eas.json's production profile env block carries only EXPO_NO_METRO_WORKSPACE_ROOT and SENTRY_DISABLE_AUTO_UPLOAD — no DSN var and no EAS secret binding. The DSN only exists in gitignored .env.local via load-repo-env.cjs, and the 90-day zero proves it never reached a shipped build. iOS is the primary product surface: a launch-week native crash, RevenueCat purchase failure, or auth wedge on real users' phones produces no signal anywhere.
findings.recommendation: Add "sentryDsn": "<super-mobile DSN>" to apps/mobile/app.json extra (DSNs are publishable client identifiers — same commit posture as the posthogKey on line 103), cut a new TestFlight build, force a test crash (Sentry.nativeCrash() behind a dev menu or a throwing test button) and confirm the event arrives in super-mobile. Then author one explicit Sentry alert on super-mobile: 'a new issue OR any event where level:fatal' → email gracehowse@outlook.com (named recipient, not ActiveMembers fallthrough).
evidenceRefs: audit2 evidence/sentry-issues.md (super-mobile: 0 events/0 spans, 90d, live MCP)

findings.title: Production runs on Supabase Free + Vercel Hobby into a 10k-user push — both fail by hard-stop, and no alarm at any tier would say so
findings.detail: Live-confirmed: Supabase org plan 'free' (get_organization, 2026-07-05) on Micro compute (57 effective connections); Vercel on Hobby (cron-migration evidence + env census). Supabase Free does not degrade — it hard-stops: 500MB DB then read-only (signups and food logs fail product-wide), ~5GB/month egress (10k users' recipe/photo-log image round-trips plausibly exceed that in day one), MAU gating on auth. Vercel Hobby is licensed non-commercial and Vercel's remedy for a viral day is pausing the project, not metered overage; the error-rate alert (Alarm 5) cannot even be configured on Hobby. Zero of the six runbook alarms detect plan-limit proximity on either vendor. This is the one failure family in the entire census where 10k users tomorrow produces a total, product-wide outage whose first signal is users telling Grace, not the reverse.
findings.recommendation: Before the viral push, in one sitting: (1) upgrade the Supabase org to Pro and enable PITR (this simultaneously closes the open 2026-06-01 pitr-posture decision — fill in the doc's Decision section); (2) upgrade Vercel to Pro (also unblocks Alarm 5); (3) same afternoon, set the vendor-side alerts that only exist on paid tiers: Supabase dashboard usage alerts (DB size 70%, egress 70%) and a Vercel error-rate + usage notification. Combined cost is roughly $45-50/month — it is the cheapest launch insurance in this entire audit.
findings.route: entire product (auth, DB, all API routes)
evidenceRefs: audit2 evidence/observability.md (Hobby-plan cron migration, Alarm 5 unconfigurable)

findings.title: Supabase advisor security cron failing every day (SUPABASE_PAT never set) — and the failure itself is silent; still failing as of this morning
findings.detail: The daily RLS/security advisor check (app/api/cron/supabase-advisor-check, Alarm 6, marked '✅ Wired' in docs/operations/alerting.md) 503s server_misconfigured on every attempt because SUPABASE_PAT was never added to Vercel production — re-confirmed absent in this pass's live env census, and the 06:00 UTC slot failed again today (run 28732775129, 2m7s = the 3×503 retry pattern; the succeeding 7-9s runs are the hourly weigh-in slots). Nothing routes a failed schedule-triggered GitHub Actions run to a human, so the alarm that exists to catch RLS regressions has been dark since at least 2026-07-02 and its darkness is also invisible. This is the codebase's most-repeated historical failure mode (monitoring gap unnoticed for weeks) recurring inside the system built to prevent it.
findings.recommendation: Two concrete steps: (1) create a Supabase personal access token, `vercel env add SUPABASE_PAT production`, redeploy, and confirm the next scheduled run goes green via `gh run watch`; (2) add a terminal `if: failure()` step to .github/workflows/scheduled-crons.yml that captures to Sentry (a one-line curl to the store endpoint with the existing SENTRY_PUBLIC_KEY) or sends email — so any future cron failure pages Grace instead of rotting in the Actions tab.
evidenceRefs: audit2 evidence/observability.md §6 (run 28732775129 full log: 3× HTTP 503 SUPABASE_PAT unset)

findings.title: No /api/healthz route and no external uptime monitor — the cheapest possible 3am answer doesn't exist
findings.detail: Verified this pass: app/api/healthz does not exist, yet the DR runbook's S5 rollback verification step and the alerting runbook's latency probe both reference it. More importantly, there is no synthetic external check of any kind — so a Vercel outage, a Supabase hard-stop (IM-04's failure mode), a bad deploy that 500s the app shell, or a DNS/cert failure has no detection path other than Sentry (which only fires if client code loads far enough to report) or a user complaint. For a solo founder, an external uptime monitor is the single highest-leverage 3am alarm: it is vendor-independent, covers whole-stack failures the per-vendor alarms structurally cannot see, and takes under an hour to stand up.
findings.recommendation: Ship app/api/healthz/route.ts (runtime nodejs; SELECT 1 against Supabase via the service client with a 3s timeout; return 200 {ok, commit: VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA} or 503) — small PR with a unit test. Then point a free external monitor (UptimeRobot or BetterStack free tier, 1-minute interval) at https://suppr.app/api/healthz with email + push alert to Grace's phone. This one action converts IM-04's 'users find out first' scenario into a page within 2 minutes.
evidenceRefs: audit2 evidence/runbooks.md §1 (S5 step references unconfirmed healthz)

findings.title: DR is a runbook, not a capability: restore never rehearsed (RTO unknown), 117 storage objects have zero byte-level backup, and ENG-510 is marked Done with its substantive checklist unchecked
findings.detail: Live-verified 2026-07-05: RPO is up to 24h (Free-tier daily backup, no PITR) and the documented 4-8h RTO is a guess — no restore has ever been executed against this project ('a restore that has never been rehearsed is a restore that doesn't exist', the runbook's own words). The recipe-images bucket (117 objects, ~48MB — every user-uploaded recipe/meal photo) has no versioning, no mirror, no recovery path for the bytes. The Pre-Phase-1 checklist's three most consequential items (rehearsal, PITR decision, storage mirror) are open, five weeks past their own deadline, and Linear ENG-510 was closed Done on the documentation deliverable alone — the exact 'silent deferral' pattern CLAUDE.md bans, hiding live risk behind a green ticket. Mobile recovery has an extra cliff: a DB restore lands on a new supabase.co ref, and mobile clients are stuck on the old URL until a new TestFlight build ships.
findings.recommendation: Three concrete actions, sequenced after IM-04's Pro upgrade: (1) run the first restore rehearsal to a scratch project within 7 days, time every step, and record it in the runbook's rehearsal log table (the quarterly calendar reminder fires 2026-07-06 — use it); (2) stand up a nightly recipe-images mirror: a scheduled GitHub Actions job using rclone/S3-compatible sync from Supabase Storage to a Cloudflare R2 bucket (~30 lines of YAML, pennies/month at 48MB); (3) reopen ENG-510 or file a successor ticket carrying the two unchecked items, so the tracker matches reality.
evidenceRefs: audit2 evidence/backup-dr.md (live storage census, ENG-510 checklist state, plan 'free')

findings.title: Billing webhook handlers are excellent but undetected-failure blind: Stripe Alarm 4 unwired, RC dashboard alert unconfirmed, no periodic entitlement reconciliation
findings.detail: The handler code passes every integration-manager check: signature verification (Stripe constructEvent; RC constant-time bearer + 26h freshness/anti-replay), DB-backed idempotency (stripe_webhook_events / revenuecat_events, 23505 dedup, fail-safe-forward), correct 500-for-retry, never-downgrade-on-partial-payload, lifetime_pro floor. The gap is purely detection and reconciliation: nothing notices a sustained webhook failure (bad deploy 500ing the route, rotated secret) until a paying user's tier visibly drifts — on mobile, the only live billing rail (per IM-02), that means a paying customer locked out of Pro with no signal. RC's dashboard delivery-failure alert is referenced in the runbook but never confirmed configured. The 'subscription state reconciled from the provider' posture exists per-event but there is no scheduled sweep comparing profiles.user_tier against RC/Stripe truth to catch anything the event stream dropped. One soft handler edge (RC skips the freshness check when event_timestamp_ms is absent) is acceptable but worth pinning in a test.
findings.recommendation: Three concrete actions: (1) in the RevenueCat dashboard, enable the webhook delivery-failure notification to gracehowse@outlook.com and screenshot it into docs/operations/revenuecat-webhook-runbook.md (10 minutes, closes the unconfirmed item); (2) when Stripe goes live (IM-02 Option A), enable Stripe Dashboard → Webhooks endpoint failure email notifications in the same sitting (Alarm 4, also free); (3) add a weekly reconciliation script (scripts/reconcile-entitlements.mjs, invoked from scheduled-crons.yml) that pulls active RC subscribers via the RC API, diffs against profiles.user_tier, and Sentry-captures any mismatch — this converts 'webhook order doesn't matter' from a per-event property into a system guarantee.
evidenceRefs: audit2 evidence/scale-hotpaths.md §3 (webhook posture, no finding on handlers)
audit2 evidence/runbooks.md §3 (Alarm 4 not wired; RC dashboard alert unconfirmed)

findings.title: Sentry alerting is an accident of defaults, and dev/local traffic pollutes the production signal — 100% of 'production' error volume is local smoke-test noise
findings.detail: Live-verified: the only alert rules on both projects are Sentry's auto-provisioned day-one defaults routing to ActiveMembers fallthrough — no named recipient, no volume/spike condition (Alarm 1's spec called for >50 events/hr), zero metric alerts org-wide, and the quota Spend Notification (Alarm 2) unwired. Meanwhile every one of suppr-web's 5 unresolved issues traces to localhost:3000/HeadlessChrome/Grace's MacBook, and the 91 events tagged environment:production over 90 days are all a local `next start` smoke test on 127.0.0.1:3100. On launch day this matters twice: a real production incident will be visually indistinguishable from the standing dev noise, and the emails Grace gets carry no urgency signal. Also: plain SENTRY_DSN is unset in Vercel (server/edge silently ride the public DSN's fallback branch — works today, breaks silently if an SDK update drops the fallback).
findings.recommendation: One Sentry afternoon: (1) on both projects, replace the default rule with an authored rule per the Alarm 1 spec — 'new issue OR >50 events/1hr in environment:production' → email gracehowse@outlook.com as a named recipient; (2) set the org Spend Notification at 70% of monthly quota (Alarm 2); (3) stop dev pollution at the source — set enabled: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' && Boolean(DSN) in the web Sentry configs (or a dedicated dev DSN), so localhost/CI traffic never reaches the prod project; (4) `vercel env add SENTRY_DSN production` to close the fallback-dependence noted in docs/observability.md line 49.
evidenceRefs: audit2 evidence/sentry-issues.md (all 5 issues dev-origin; 91 prod-tagged events = local smoke test)
audit2 evidence/observability.md §1,3 (default rules, no metric alerts, SENTRY_DSN gap)

findings.title: AI + image-gen circuit breakers are the best guardrails in the stack but are sized for 1k DAU — a 10k day trips them within hours; enforcement value and falBudget's Redis fail-policy unconfirmed
findings.detail: The Anthropic/OpenAI breaker (per-user 50/day + global £50/day, 70% alarm, reserve/commit/release, fail-open-5min-then-closed) and the fal.ai breaker (£10/day + £150/month, enforcement ON by default) are genuinely production-grade. But the £50/day AI cap was explicitly sized for 1k DAU (decision doc's own math: ~£25/day); linear scaling to 10k DAU is ~£250/day, tripping the global cap in ~2 hours of a launch spike — by design, but it means AI photo/voice logging 503s mid-launch-day unless the cap is consciously pre-widened. fal.ai's £10/day ≈ 90 images: 5% of 10k users importing one recipe trips it 5×+ on day one. Partially closed this pass: AI_BUDGET_ENFORCEMENT_ENABLED IS present in Vercel production (set 18d ago, matching the documented 2026-06-17 flip) — but the encrypted value ('true' required; code default false per aiBudget.ts:198) is unconfirmed. Open sub-gap from the census: falBudget.ts's Redis helpers lack the try/catch fail-policy aiBudget.ts has, so an Upstash blip during reserveFalImageBudget likely throws uncaught into the image-hero route.
findings.recommendation: Three actions: (1) `vercel env pull` (or dashboard peek) to confirm AI_BUDGET_ENFORCEMENT_ENABLED=true — 2 minutes, closes P1-9 for good; (2) make a deliberate launch-week cap decision NOW and write it in docs/decisions/: e.g. AI_BUDGET_GLOBAL_DAILY_GBP=250 and FAL_BUDGET_DAILY_GBP=50 for the push window (the 90-second env-toggle redeploy is already documented as the widen path — decide the number before 3am, not during); (3) small PR wrapping falBudget.ts Redis calls in the same fail-open/closed policy as aiBudget.ts, with a unit test simulating Upstash unreachable.

findings.title: Food-vendor quota cliffs at 10k users: Edamam (1k/day) is decorative, FatSecret (10k/day) exhaustible by midnight, USDA (~900/hr) trips in a novel-query spike — and no alarm fires when a vendor degrades out of the chain
findings.detail: The degrade-to-next-source architecture (90% quota trip, 24h Redis cache, per-user throttles, bounded retries, differentiated fail-open) is exactly the right resilience design and is the strongest integration pattern in the codebase. But the caps behind it are pre-launch-sized: Edamam's 1,000/day free tier is guaranteed exhausted within the first hour at any real scale — permanently degraded out of the chain every day (independently flagged by the 2026-06-22 audit); FatSecret's 10,000/day equals 10k users × 1 call with zero headroom; USDA's assumed 1,000/hr (never re-verified against the live key's tier) trips in under 20 minutes of a viral spike of novel queries. Net user effect is silent nutrition-quality erosion — branded-food coverage and autocomplete quietly narrow to USDA/OFF for most of every day — the exact 'trust-label' quality Grace flagged as never-defer. No signal exists when a vendor enters degraded state: the vendorSearchCache knows, but tells no one. OFF's alleged cache bypass is still carried unverified.
findings.recommendation: Before the push: (1) buy the Edamam paid tier (usage-priced $0.00003/req per the client's own doc comment — a 10k-user day costs cents) or explicitly remove Edamam from the source chain and document it — a permanently-degraded vendor is dead weight plus latency; (2) email FatSecret about Premier paid-tier limits and re-verify the USDA key's actual hourly cap (both are one-email/one-dashboard checks; record findings in docs/decisions/); (3) add a fireAlarmOnce-style Sentry warning in vendorSearchCache.ts when any vendor stays quota-degraded >30min ('vendor X degraded out of the search chain'), mirroring the aiBudget 70% alarm pattern already proven in this codebase; (4) close the OFF cache-bypass question with a 15-minute route read and either fix or mark not-a-gap.

findings.title: No auth-outage runbook — Sign in with Apple is the only mobile auth path and an Apple-side SIWA outage locks out every mobile user with no written play
findings.detail: The runbook census found written plays for 4 of the 5 likely incident classes (DB down, bad deploy, webhook outage, quota exhaustion) — several reinforced by fail-safe code. Auth is the missing fifth: no DR scenario, no founder-safety-net entry, no alarm covers either (a) Supabase Auth degrading while Postgres stays up (distinct managed service, distinct failure domain) or (b) Apple's SIWA service degrading — a vendor Suppr doesn't operate, sitting in front of the only mobile sign-in path. During a launch spike this is the worst-timed lockout: new users can't sign up, existing users bounced from sessions can't get back in, and Grace has no trigger to distinguish 'Apple is down' from 'we shipped an auth bug' quickly.
findings.recommendation: Write docs/runbooks/auth-outage.md (half a day): detection signals (Apple System Status page for 'Sign in with Apple', status.supabase.com Auth component, Sentry auth-error fingerprint spike once IM-03 lands), a decision tree separating Apple-side vs Supabase-side vs Suppr-side, comms templates, and reuse of the existing dr_full_outage_banner flag with auth-specific copy. Add both status pages to the founder-safety-net vendor inventory with 'subscribe to status emails' as a checklist item — Supabase and Apple both offer them free.
evidenceRefs: audit2 evidence/runbooks.md §4,7 (auth outage: 'No — the one real gap')
apps/mobile/CLAUDE.md (Apple Sign In only auth path)

findings.title: PostHog session-replay sample rate defaults to 1.0 (100%) with the documented dashboard flip to 0.1 unverified — plus a next-launch-only propagation lag on mobile
findings.detail: DEFAULT_SESSION_REPLAY_SAMPLE_RATE = 1.0 confirmed in code this pass (sessionReplaySampleRate.ts:38); the module's own comment plans a pre-launch dashboard flip to 0.1 that no evidence confirms happened. With web PostHog currently dead (IM-01) the burn is mobile-only today — but the moment IM-01 is fixed AND traffic arrives, 100% replay capture on both platforms is the fastest quota burn in the census (replay gets cut first at the hard cap, silently). Mobile adds a lag: the sample rate is cached in AsyncStorage and applies on the user's NEXT app launch, so a mid-spike dashboard change doesn't bite until each user relaunches — the flip has to happen before the push, not during it.
findings.recommendation: Set the session-replay sample-rate flag payload to 0.1 in the PostHog dashboard (project 389168) before the push and record the change in docs/decisions/2026-05-13-session-replay-and-feature-flags.md. Sequence it with IM-01's fix so web comes online already sampled at 0.1. Keep 1.0 only while N=1 remains literally true.

findings.title: Two forward-looking scale bombs on the hot path: unbounded `saves` fetch on every session boot (web + mobile) and per-ingredient unindexed ilike scans in the core nutrition matcher
findings.detail: (1) Every authenticated boot loads the user's ENTIRE saves history with no .limit()/.range() — AppDataContext.tsx:1664-1667, apps/mobile/lib/recipes.ts:303-306/466-470/516-524 — while Base/Pro is explicitly unlimited-saves. Degrades smoothly and invisibly as retained paying users (the exact cohort being optimised for) accumulate saves; fixing later requires a client pagination retrofit, not a query tweak. (2) verifyIngredients.ts:627 fires a leading-wildcard ilike against user_foods.name once per ingredient (8 concurrent) inside every recipe import / photo-log / voice-log — no pg_trgm/GIN index exists (migration grep + live advisor corroboration), so each is a sequential scan on a table whose purpose is to grow via crowdsourcing. Failure mode is 'AI logging feels slow' creeping in with no error, no deploy correlation, no alert. Neither is a day-one outage; both are the cheapest-now, expensive-later class, and the codebase has fixed this exact shape twice before (ENG-705 journal window, T23 webhook dedup).
findings.recommendation: Two Linear tickets, both label:launch-blocker-adjacent not blocking: (1) migration `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm; CREATE INDEX ... ON user_foods USING gin (name gin_trgm_ops);` via `supabase db push --linked`, then EXPLAIN ANALYZE to confirm the planner picks it — no app-code change; (2) cap the saves id fetch at .limit(500) now (covers any realistic user for months) and design the cursor/hasMore contract for the Library surface while the table is small.

findings.title: Last night's full CI gate is red: 6 web tests failing across 4 files, including the Stripe checkout integration gate — triage before any launch push
findings.detail: ci-report.txt (stage1, full `npm run ci`): Test Files 4 failed | 955 passed; Tests 6 failed | 9781 passed. All six are 5000ms timeouts — tests/integration/stripeCheckoutRoute.test.ts (the 401-gate integration test), tests/unit/dayStripPagerStrideWeb.test.tsx (×3), tests/unit/foodSearchFitThisIn.test.tsx, tests/unit/onboardingSignupSessionGate.test.tsx — a signature consistent with the documented CPU-contention flake mode (full suite alongside a workflow), not necessarily real regressions. But the project's own rule is merge/ship only on GREEN, and one of the failing files gates the billing route this audit already flags (IM-02). An additional unhandled ENOENT on coverage/.tmp closed the run.
findings.recommendation: Re-run the four failing files in isolation on an idle machine: `npx vitest run tests/integration/stripeCheckoutRoute.test.ts tests/unit/dayStripPagerStrideWeb.test.tsx tests/unit/foodSearchFitThisIn.test.tsx tests/unit/onboardingSignupSessionGate.test.tsx`. If green in isolation, record the flake and raise testTimeout on the four (or gate them with vi.useFakeTimers per the CLAUDE.md date-test guidance); if any fails isolated, fix before push. Either way, get one fully-green `npm run ci` on record before the viral push.

findings.title: TestFlight 90-day build-expiry cliff guarded only by a human calendar reminder — Alarm 10 (expiry poll) never built
findings.detail: TestFlight builds hard-expire at 90 days; if the only promoted build lapses, no one can install the app at exactly the moment a viral push is driving installs. The founder-safety-net policy ('never exceed ~75 days', 60-day calendar check) is a human-memory control; the robust fix — a scheduled job polling the App Store Connect API for expirationDate and alerting under 7 days — is Alarm 10, status 'Not yet wired'. The repo already has working ASC API auth in scripts/fetch-testflight-feedback.mjs, so the marginal cost is small.
findings.recommendation: Add a weekly job to .github/workflows/scheduled-crons.yml running a new scripts/check-testflight-expiry.mjs (reuse the ASC credentials/JWT pattern from scripts/fetch-testflight-feedback.mjs): fetch the latest build's expirationDate; if <14 days, fail the run loudly (which, once IM-05's failure-notification step lands, pages Grace). Retire the calendar reminder once the alarm has fired correctly in a dry run.
evidenceRefs: audit2 evidence/runbooks.md §2 (expiry cliff, Alarm 10 not wired)
